dialogos.texto_en: Miku, Itsuki.\nWhat are you looking at?
Ah, Futaro.
I'm not sure about Nino,\nbut I don't think it's something\nthat would interest you, Uesugi-kun...
What do you mean? You just made me curious.
No, I didn't mean it in a bad way.\nI just thought that you're not someone\nwho would be interested in cosmetics.
Cosmetics?
Oh, cosmetics, yeah, cosmetics.\nIt depends on the person, but I don't hate cosmetics.
That's the same reaction I got from you when you were \nspeaking about aromas.
Those are basically skincare products.\nIt's not food.
Then why use a foreign word to describe it?
Nino, this one looks good.\nIt's a facial toner made with hibiscus.
Oh, I've never seen that before.\nThere's such a thing?
This shop carries cosmetics\nmade with regional products.
How about it?\nWould you like to try it too, Uesugi-kun?
...Nah, I'll be alright.
There's also a beauty oil that smells really good.\nIt seems to be made with shikuwasa.
Woah! This citrus scent is amazing.\nI thought it was something to eat.
Please don't eat it, okay?
I'm not gonna eat it! I just thought about it!
They also sell bath salts.\nThese are made with natural salt from Okinawa.
(Now it's salt.\n How is it different from food?)
It also contains herbs from Okinawa.\nLet's see here...an herb called gettou?\nHmm, I've never heard of this either.
(Now, herbs...)
Facial toner, or oils...\nI want to buy one, but which one is better?
Since you're already here,\nwhy not buy both?
But I still have some that I'm using.
It's not something that spoils easily,\nso you could buy it now\nand use it later, couldn't you?
Ah! This soap is nice too.\nIt's made with seaweed\nand makes your skin smooth, they say.
I'm interested in that too.\nApparently combining this soap with the mud pack here\nis good for your skin.
(They're really getting into this...\n It's a world I can't keep up with anymore.)
I know this one!\nThis is aroma! Hey, Nino! This is aroma, isn't it?
Yes, yes, it's an aroma candle.\nYou remembered it well.
He's so happy, just from remembering that...
Was he feeling lonely\nbecause he couldn't join in on the conversation?
The one I gave to you before, Futaro,\nhad a fragrance that helps with fatigue recovery%p-2;――
This series seems to have a scent inspired by the sun, sea, \nand sky of Okinawa.
Hey, don't you want to get this one, Futaro?
Even if I did, beats me how to use it...
I taught you how to use it before, didn't I?\nDoesn't it help you relax when you use it?
To be honest, I don't know if it really works.\nMaybe it's just not for me.
Is there really such a thing as being suited\nor not suited for aromatherapy?
I wonder...\nIf you're not interested in scents,\nthen maybe it's not for you.
But you did like the scent, didn't you?
Well, yeah. It wasn't bad or anything.
Okay then, let me choose for you.\nForget about the relaxation part\nand let's just enjoy the scent.
Looking at the flame calms me down.\nThe flickering light draws you in.
Are you sure you're using it correctly?\nAromatherapy candles are meant to be enjoyed\nfor their scent.
Really?\nWell, I guess the scent wasn't bad either.
It's okay to enjoy the ambiance of the flickering flame.\nIt's also part of the experience.
It's nice to just relax\nand watch the burning candle in the bath, too.
In the bath, huh?\nI didn't think of using it like that.
If you like watching the flame, then let's buy one!\nI'll choose something that I think you'll like, Futaro.
Sure, you pick one out for me.
Leave it to me!\nWhat kind of scent do you prefer?
Scent, eh.\nThe one I got before\nthat had a relaxing effect wasn't bad.
Okay, how about something with a sweeter scent\nthan that? Do you have a preference?
I'm not sure about something too sweet.\nI just want something\nthat won't interfere with my studies.
Hmm, then how about something with a component\nthat can help boost concentration?\nI'd say, a mild scent...
Sorry. Pretty vague instructions, huh.
No, it's fine.\nIt's fun to learn more about your preferences like this,\nFutaro.
Futaro,\nit's amazing how you do these things unconsciously.
You forgot about our presence, didn't you.
If you're looking for relaxation effects,\nthen lavender, rose, jasmine...
Oh, and gettou too, it seems.
It is also good for improving concentration and memory...\nOh yes, it's perfect for you, Futaro.
(Nino seems to be having fun.\n I'm glad I decided to let her choose.)
(...Hm?\n Wait, does it mean that\n I'm not the one taking the lead?)
(Pull yourself together, man!)
